title: osmorhiza glabrata
intro: Osmorhiza glabrata, also known as sweet cicely or American sweet cicely, is a perennial plant native to eastern North America, characterized by its fern-like leaves and umbrella-like inflorescences. The plant's roots, rhizomes, and roots' extracts have been used in traditional medicine for their antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and carminative properties. Consuming Osmorhiza glabrata has been associated with various health benefits, including relief from digestive issues, reduced inflammation, and potential antimicrobial effects. In herbal preparations, Osmorhiza glabrata is often used in teas, tinctures, and infusions, and is traditionally paired with sweetener plants like licorice root to mask its bitter flavor.
